text: Grayscale: Hyperliquid Is the Breakout Success Story of Modern Digital Assets Grayscale, one of the world's largest digital asset managers, said Hyperliquid has become "the breakout success story of the modern digital assets industry" and one of the fastest-growing DeFi trading platforms. The report said Hyperliquid, focused on perpetual futures, handled about $2.9 trillion in perps volume in 2025 and now has roughly $7 billion in open interest, making it the third- or fourth-largest crypto perpetuals platform globally. Grayscale also noted that HYPE's annualized volatility is about twice that of Bitcoin, while Hyperliquid's validator set remains relatively concentrated and its core software is still closed-source. — link

text: I've been following your trades since 2024, and I don't understand your attempts to portray yourself as a trader who trades the way others do. Most of your trades are skillful short-term plays on a strong trend with a comfortable risk-reward ratio, You're like a monkey trying to swim like a fish instead of climbing trees. Everyone has their own trading style, and trying to conform to some standard is like being a beta on a memecoin. I fed my trades to an AI assistant and saw that the root cause of my mistakes is one thing, and the root cause of my successes is another; these trades don't overlap in meaning at all. I was losing money simply because I was trying to be like some successful person in this field, but this is a personal journey—to each their own. Translated using DeepL; my English isn't good enough to write decent texts.

text: Dear Crypto Passengers, This is the Last Stop of This Train The original dream of crypto (a bottom-up, permissionless financial revolution led by retail users) is dead. Instead of decentralized networks taking over Wall Street, traditional financial institutions, banks, and payment giants have co-opted the underlying blockchain technology to build a regulated, highly efficient Digital Asset Economy • The retail crowd largely treated crypto as a speculative casino, trading memecoins and losing interest during market downturns • Traditional institutions realized blockchain is the most efficient way for moving money • The space has transitioned from a fringe cypherpunk movement into its final evolutionary stage: an invisible engine powering global fintech
author: admin#1742094092

text: The Bull Case for Collectibles High-end collectibles like watches and sports memorabilia are no longer just niche hobbies or status symbols; they have transformed into "stories with supply constraints." Driven by global internet communities, a new generation of highly educated, culturally aware buyers now tracks, discusses, and values these items much like stocks or real estate Microscopic Supply vs. Exploding Wealth: While global wealth and tech-driven liquidity are expanding rapidly, the supply of top-tier, culturally significant items remains strictly fixed. Because these ultra-rare items act as "Schelling points" where global wealth converges to own recognized history, their rapid price discovery is beginning to mirror the multi-century-old fine art market

text: It's been almost a year since I wrote this article. And nearly 2 years since I first started believing - and endlessly posting - that Hyperliquid would become the house of finance. Now, it actually is. A lot of projects have already faded away, but many OG protocols are still here building every day: Unit, Trade, Kinetiq, HyperLend, RedStone, Valantis, Pear Protocol, LiquidLaunch, WalletV, Hyperwave, Hybra Finance, Nest Exchange, HyperSwap, Project X, Insilico Trading, and Liminal Money. At the same time, a new wave of builders is creating even more opportunities across the ecosystem: Monetrix, Papertrade, alt.fun, Nova Markets, Perps.fun. So if you're still curious about why Hyperliquid matters, how to get started, or what's happening in the ecosystem, feel free to check out the article. I'll be updating it soon - removing outdated info and adding more alpha along the way. In the meantime, you can find almost everything you need at hl.eco. It's never too late to be part of Hyperliquid. Article:

text: South Korea's Crypto Trading Activity Falls to One-Tenth of Stock Market Levels According to Digital Asset, following the sharp decline in the digital asset market in October 2025 and the continued record highs of South Korea's KOSPI index, trading activity in the country's crypto market has fallen to roughly one-tenth of stock market levels. Data shows that in May this year, total cryptocurrency trading volume across South Korea's five licensed exchanges — Upbit, Bithumb, Coinone, Korbit, and Gopax — accounted for only 8% of KOSPI stock market trading volume during the same period. By comparison, the ratio had surged to 323% in December 2024 following the crypto bull market triggered by Donald Trump's election victory in the United States. — link

text: South Korea Makes First Arrest and Prosecution in DEX Rug Pull Case According to Digital Asset, South Korean prosecutors charged a criminal group accused of manipulating the price of the Solana-based meme coin CATFI and generating KRW 400 million (~ $260,000) in illegal profits, marking the country's first arrest and prosecution related to a DEX rug pull case. Investigators said the main suspect, surnamed Park, posed on social media as influencer "Eth Father" and falsely promoted CATFI as an independent third party before executing a rug pull that caused KRW 900 million in losses to 256 investors. — link

text: Binance will cease support for deposits and withdrawals of several tokens via specified networks starting from June 3, 2026, at 08:00. The affected digital assets and their respective networks include: DODO (DODO) via Arbitrum and BNB Chain; Heima (HEI) via BNB Chain; Hashflow (HFT) via Arbitrum , Solana, and BNB Chain; Synapse (SYN) via Arbitrum, Solana, Polygon, Optimism, Avalanche C-Chain, Fantom, and BNB Chain; and Alien Worlds (TLM) via BNB Chain. — link

text: The Pin Factory Trap - Hyper-Specialisation and Its Faults in the Age of AI While the division of labor historically drove modern prosperity, hyper-specialisation has become a dangerous trap because AI models now excel at well-defined, narrow knowledge tasks, stripping away the premium and security once provided by deep, isolated credentials The New Playbook for Survival: To avoid becoming obsolete, professionals must balance depth with breadth by maintaining a systems-thinking view, understanding how different domains connect, building human relationships, and ensuring they have "skin in the game" to pivot before their field goes stale

text: Highlight Clip CZ: If I Could Do It Again, I'd Block U.S. Users From Day One Binance founder Changpeng Zhao (CZ) reflected in a May 9, 2026 interview with Crypto In America that if he could do it again, he would block U.S. users and set up geo-fencing from day one, while investing earlier in KYC and compliance. He said Binance was only a roughly 20-person team with very few users in 2017, and he viewed it more as a technology platform than a financial platform at the time. CZ said many of the later problems could have been avoided at the source if those steps had been taken from the beginning. — link
